democrat.note: EC-adjusted V_eq derived via logistic regression on (popular_2p_share → EC_outcome) across all 20 cycles 1948-2024. Threshold = popular-vote share where P(Dem EC win) = 0.50. Accounts for geographic inefficiency: Dems lost EC with 50.3% (2000) and 51.1% (2016). Recompute via derive_ec_veq('democrat') in electoral/models/ml_baseline.py.

republican.note: EC-adjusted V_eq = 1 - dem_threshold. Republicans won EC with 49.7% (2000), reflecting geographic efficiency advantage. Expressed as Republican two-party share (1 - dem_share) to match the optimizer's coordinate system. Recompute via derive_ec_veq('republican') in electoral/models/ml_baseline.py.

_methodology: Logistic regression: P(Dem EC win) = sigmoid(A * dem_2p_share + B), fit on 20 cycles, C=1e4 (no regularisation). Threshold = -B/A. See DECISIONS.md §GP Classifier for derivation rationale.
